title: SQLi Testing Methodology
when_to_use: When testing web applications for SQLi

steps.title: Initial Setup/Scanning
steps.instruction: Test form inputs and URL parameters with basic SQL injection payloads like ' OR 1=1-- -
steps.tip: Start with the most reliable information source for sqli testing methodology
steps.expected_output: Application behavior changes indicating potential SQL injection vulnerability

steps.title: Data Collection/Configuration
steps.instruction: Analyse error messages to determine database type (MySQL, MSSQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle)
steps.tip: Organize data systematically to avoid duplicates and ensure coverage
steps.expected_output: Identified database type from error messages or application responses

steps.title: Analysis/Enumeration
steps.instruction: Test database column count using ORDER BY clause with incrementing numbers
steps.tip: Look for patterns and correlations in collected data
steps.expected_output: Confirmed number of columns in the vulnerable query

steps.title: Advanced Technique/Filtering
steps.instruction: Use UNION SELECT to extract data from database tables in structured format
steps.tip: Use multiple techniques to increase coverage and accuracy
steps.expected_output: Retrieved data displayed in application output confirming UNION injection works

steps.title: Correlation/Testing
steps.instruction: Extract database version, user, and schema information using version functions
steps.tip: Validate findings across different tools and methods
steps.expected_output: Confirmed database version, current user, and available databases

steps.title: Validation/Exploitation
steps.instruction: Use sqlmap to automate SQLi exploitation and database enumeration
steps.expected_output: Complete database enumeration including tables, columns, and dumped data

steps.title: Documentation/Reporting
steps.instruction: Document every injection attempt with full payloads and screenshots
steps.tip: Review all findings before compilation for accuracy and completeness
steps.expected_output: Comprehensive documentation of SQLi vulnerability with technical proof of concept
